body:
Title: CRA Form SK428 and Worksheet SK428 2025 Saskatchewan resident tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5008-c/5008-c-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5008-c/5008-c-25e.pdf
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5008-d/5008-d-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5008-d/5008-d-25e.pdf

CRA Form SK428 2025 calculates Saskatchewan tax for Saskatchewan residents. Part A calculates Saskatchewan tax on taxable income from line 26000. The bracket thresholds are $53,463 and $152,750. The rates are 10.5%, 12.5%, and 14.5%. The base tax amounts are $0.00, $5,613.62, and $18,024.49.

Part B calculates Saskatchewan non-refundable tax credits. Line 58040 basic personal amount is $19,491. Worksheet SK428 line 58080 age amount has a maximum of $5,785, starts reducing above $43,066, is zero at $81,633 or more, and uses a 15% reduction rate. Lines 58120 and 58160 use a base amount of $21,440 and a maximum of $19,491 for the spouse or common-law partner amount and eligible dependant amount after subtracting the spouse or dependant net income. Line 58210 multiplies dependent children born in 2007 or later by $7,704. Line 58220 senior supplementary amount is $2,028. Line 58357 caps the first-time home buyers' amount at $15,000. Line 58440 disability amount for self is $13,986 unless the under-18 worksheet applies. Line 58689 medical expenses are reduced by the lesser of $2,681 and 3% of net income, then medical expenses for other dependants line 58729 are added. Line 58840 multiplies the total claim amount by 10.5%. Donations and gifts line 58969 multiplies federal Schedule 9 line 13 by 10.5% and line 14 by 14.5%. Line 61500 adds line 58840 and line 58969 as Saskatchewan non-refundable tax credits.

Part C calculates Saskatchewan tax. Line 60 subtracts the farm and small business capital gains tax credit and floors negative amounts at zero. Line 62 adds Saskatchewan tax on split income line 61510. Worksheet SK428 line 61520 calculates the Saskatchewan dividend tax credit by multiplying taxable dividends other than eligible dividends line 12010 by 2.519% and taxable dividends line 12000 minus line 12010 by 11%. Line 61540 multiplies federal minimum tax carryover line 40427 by 50%. Lines 66 and 67 subtract non-refundable credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover from tax and floor negative amounts at zero. Line 68 multiplies Form T691 Part 7 line 1 minus line 2 by 50%. Line 71 subtracts provincial foreign tax credit. Line 73 calculates the Saskatchewan political contribution tax credit: contributions over $1,275 receive $650; otherwise the first $400 receives 75%, the next range to $750 receives 50% plus $300, and amounts over $750 receive 33.33% plus $475. Lines 74 to 82 subtract political contribution credit, labour-sponsored venture capital tax credit capped at $875, Saskatchewan mineral exploration tax credit, and Saskatchewan graduate tuition tax credit. Line 82 enters Saskatchewan tax on line 42800 of the T1 return. Normalized numeric values encoded from SK428 and Worksheet SK428 include 53463, 152750, 0.105, 0.125, 0.145, 5613.62, 18024.49, 19491, 5785, 43066, 81633, 0.15, 21440, 7704, 2028, 15000, 13986, 2681, 0.03, 0.50, 0.02519, 0.11, 400, 750, 1275, 650, 0.75, 0.3333, 300, 475, and 875.

heading: CRA Form SK428 and Worksheet SK428 2025 Saskatchewan Resident Tax
